title: Plays
content:
Alas, how soon the hours are over
Counted us out to play the lover!
And how much narrower is the stage
Allotted us to play the sage!
But when we play the fool, how wide                
The theatre expands! beside,
How long the audience sits before us!
How many prompters! what a chorus!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Walter Savage Landor, titled "Plays", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
